item.title: Why Am I Watching People Get Their Medical Results?
item.description: What was once discussed with a doctor is now frequently encountered first as decontextualized data on a screen.

item.title: The Hair-Loss Drug Rewriting the Rules of Masculinity
item.description: A pill to cure baldness is changing the way men age — and how they see themselves.

item.title: My Brother Keeps Falling for Online Romance Scams. Should I Bail Him Out Again?
item.description: When I hesitate to give him money, he guilts me.

item.title: Vegetative Patients May Be More Aware Than We Knew
item.description: New research is upending what we thought about the consciousness of patients, leaving families with agonizing choices.

item.title: How Did ‘Gatekeeping’ Become a Cardinal Sin?
item.description: We decided that filtering information was malicious. That didn’t mean we stopped doing it.

item.title: The Next Phase of the Immigration Crackdown Is Quieter — and More Destabilizing
item.description: Self-deportation is an idea with deep roots. It’s making a comeback.

item.title: Forks in the Dishwasher — Tines Up or Down?
item.description: A ruling on a timeless marital dispute.

item.title: Lena Dunham Is Still Trying to Figure Out Why People Hated Her So Much
item.description: The writer, actor and lightning rod is not done sharing yet.

item.title: We Need Lena Dunham Now More Than Ever
item.description: The era of “Girls” is long gone. So why are we still so fascinated by its creator?

item.title: Should You Procrastinate Productively — or by Having Fun?
item.description: A ruling on a dispute over the proper method of avoidance.

item.title: ‘I Don’t Know If We Can Come Back From This’: The View From Inside Trump’s D.H.S.
item.description: Dozens of agents and officials share their stories about working in the Department of Homeland Security during the harsh crackdown on illegal immigration.

item.title: My Longtime Housekeeper’s Work Is Slipping. What Should I Do?
item.description: We know she depends on this income, and we have hesitated to raise the issue with her.

item.title: We Don’t Really Know How A.I. Works. That’s a Problem.
item.description: For us to trust it on certain subjects, researchers in the growing field of interpretability might need to learn how to open the black box of its brain.

item.title: What We Lose When Everything Is ‘-Coded’
item.description: On the social internet, our fascination with analyzing the hidden messages in our culture has been flattened into one word.
